zh.Stoke Ash, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可斯托克 艾什
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Bacon於1537年出生在英國素福爾可盧包時，她的父親Henry Bacon21歲，母親Mrs Alice Bacon21歲。她和John Aldrich至少有4個兒子和6個女兒。她去世於1620年2月在她的家鄉享年83歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可盧包。

zh-hans.Stoke Ash, Suffolk, England: 英国素福爾可斯托克 艾什
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Bacon于1537年出生在英国素福爾可盧包时，她的父亲Henry Bacon21岁，母亲Mrs Alice Bacon21岁。她和John Aldrich至少有4个儿子和6个女儿。她去世于1620年2月在她的家乡享年83岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可盧包。

nameMeanings.name: Bacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Norman French personal name Bacun, derived from the ancient Germanic name Bac(c)o, Bahho, based on the element bag ‘(to) fight, (to) dispute’. The name was relatively common among the Normans in the form Bacus, of which the oblique case was Bacon.

English and French: from Middle English, Old French bacun, bacon ‘bacon’ (a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to Back 3), probably a metonymic occupational name for a preparer and seller of cured pork.

History: Gilles Bacon from Normandy, France, is documented in Quebec City, QC, in 1647. — Michael Bacon from England arrived in Dedham, MA, in 1640. Nathanial Bacon, from Stratton, Cornwall, arrived in Barnstaple, MA, in 1639. Another Nathaniel Bacon (1647–76), from Friston Hall, Suffolk, emigrated to VA and settled at Curl's Neck on the James river.

variants.name: Bagg
meanings: from Middle English bagge ‘bag, pack, bundle’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of bags and sacks of various kinds, including wallets and purses. It might also have been used for a beggar, or for one who carried their belongings in a bag.
from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago (Bac(c)o, Bahho), from the root bag- ‘to fight’ (see Bacon 2).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Bacon was born in 1537, in Rumburgh, Suffolk, England, her father, Henry Bacon, was 21 and her mother, Mrs Alice Bacon, was 21. She had at least 4 sons and 6 daughters with John Aldrich. She died in February 1620, in her hometown, at the age of 83, and was buried in Rumburgh, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Bacon est née en 1537, à Rumburgh, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Henry Bacon, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Mrs Alice Bacon, avait 21 ans. Elle a eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles avec John Aldrich. Elle est décédée en février 1620, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 83 ans, et a été enterrée à Rumburgh, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Bacon 1537 in Rumburgh, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry Bacon 21 und ihre Mutter Mrs Alice Bacon 21. Sie hatte mit John Aldrich mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb im Februar 1620 im Alter von 83 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Rumburgh, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Bacon è nata il nel 1537 nel Rumburgh, Suffolk, England, sua padre, Henry Bacon, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Mrs Alice Bacon, aveva 21 anni. Lei avuto almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie con John Aldrich. Morì nel febbraio 1620, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 83 anni, e fu sepolta a Rumburgh, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Baconが1537年にイギリススフォルクルンブルグで生まれた時，父Henry Baconは21歳，母Mrs Alice Baconは21歳であった。彼女はJohn Aldrichとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1620年2月に故郷で83歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクルンブルグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Bacon(이)가 1537년 영국 수폴크 럼버그h에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry Bacon(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs Alice Bacon(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 John Aldrich(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1620년 2월 그녀의 고향에서 83세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 럼버그h에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Bacon nasceu em 1537, em Rumburgh, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry Bacon, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Mrs Alice Bacon, tinha 21 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas com John Aldrich. Ela faleceu em fevereiro de 1620, em sua cidade natal, com 83 anos, e foi sepultada em Rumburgh, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Bacon родилась в 1537 г. в местности румбург, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Henry Bacon было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Mrs Alice Bacon, 21. У неё было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей от John Aldrich. Она умерла февраль 1620 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 83 лет и была похоронена в местности румбург, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Bacon nació en 1537, en Rumburgh, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry Bacon, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Mrs Alice Bacon, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas con John Aldrich. Murió en febrero de 1620, en su pueblo, a la edad de 83 años, y fue sepultada en Rumburgh, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
